source.title: Limits and constraints · Cloudflare Browser Rendering docs
source.acquisition: overview artifact plus targeted Context7 doc query against /websites/developers_cloudflare_browser-rendering

summary: Browser Rendering has explicit limits and timeout behavior, and the account-level session budget is not infinite. The correct operator posture is reuse-first, targeted, and skeptical of brute-force reruns.

signals: The Browser Rendering docs include dedicated Limits and REST API timeouts pages.
The Puppeteer docs expose a limits object with activeSessions, allowedBrowserAcquisitions, maxConcurrentSessions, and timeUntilNextAllowedBrowserAcquisition.

repoBehavior: Repeated create attempts can become the wrong move faster than the optimistic plan says they should.
The repo workflow already benefits from staging and reusing a successful crawl artifact instead of blindly creating a new job.
observed.certaintyNote: These are repo-level operating observations, not claims about undocumented Cloudflare numeric ceilings.

practicalGuidance: Concurrency is account-bounded; new browser acquisition can be temporarily blocked even when the workflow is otherwise valid.
Timeouts are a first-class part of the REST API surface and should be treated as normal operating constraints, not edge cases.
Beta endpoints and crawl-heavy runs deserve extra skepticism because rate/limit weirdness is real, not imaginary busywork.
Reuse successful local crawl outputs before reissuing create calls.
Prefer targeted acquisition over broad reruns when building tracked knowledge.
Keep the tracked corpus concise so it reflects known-good facts instead of padded speculation.

cleaning.notes: This file is intentionally conservative. It separates documented signals, repo-observed behavior, and practical guidance so undocumented numeric ceilings do not get smuggled in as facts.
